old_claim.note: Shamanism & Animism provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct edge remains because Jericho plastered-skull evidence supports ancestor figures, community forebears, and an ancestor-cult interpretation, not a shamanic mediator or generic animistic spirit-world dependency.

source_shape.source_locator: The British Museum describes the skulls as likely ancestor figures and portraits of community forebears without identifying shamans, shamanic mediation, or a generic animist belief system.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports ancestor-veneration scope but not the shamanism_animism prerequisite.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Keeping the edge would launder a broader spirit-mediation model into a Jericho-specific ancestor-cult claim.

ontology_before: Ancestor Worship depended directly on Shamanism & Animism through a generic expert-inference edge.
ontology_after: Neolithic Ancestor Veneration depends directly on kinship systems only; shamanism remains a separate broad belief-system node for cases with evidence of spirit mediation.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: shamanism_animism is no longer a direct prerequisite for ancestor_worship.
would_reject_if: A Jericho plastered-skull source directly identified shamanic mediation as required for the ancestor-cult interpretation.
The node were intentionally broadened to a comparative anthropology category covering many spirit-world practices.
Downstream nodes depended on this edge and needed a shamanism path preserved.
short_reason: The reviewed Jericho evidence does not identify shamanic mediation as a direct prerequisite.
